FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Klarna pursues U.S. bank charter while regional banks pursue tuck-in M&A; regulatory pressure intensifies on undercapitalized lenders.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for ILC charter with FDIC, pivoting from pure BNPL to full deposit-taking and lending platform under U.S. regulation.
Regions Financial acquires muni bond specialist Frazer Lanier to build out municipal underwriting—classic bolt-on consistent with stated M&A strategy.
Fed issues third enforcement action against Kansas-based Small Business Bank for undercapitalization, demanding 30-day remediation—escalating pressure on struggling lenders.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Wealth management consolidation and platform evolution driving dealmaking: Vestmark hits $50B AUM milestone, Empower acquires Milliman for $340M, while some advisors opt out of the roll-up wave.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP platform exploded past $50B AUM (5x growth) after launching standalone UMA with tax-loss harvesting, triggering referral deals from T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, and VanEck.
Empower acquired Milliman for $340M, adding 1.53M retirement plan participants and enabling wealth cross-sell into workplace benefits—classic scale play in DC/DB space.

Aspen Standard bought an undisclosed Michigan RIA for $1.3B, hitting $15B AUM across eight affiliates; meanwhile, Integrated Partners counters consolidation trend by recruiting $4B in AUM as a PE-free autonomy play.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Cat bond market roaring with record Q2 issuance; climate risks forcing regulatory scrutiny and M&A consolidation in P&C.

bullets.text: Cat bond issuance hit $11.3B in Q2—a quarterly record across 48 deals, signaling strong investor appetite for catastrophe risk transfer.
EU banking regulator developing heat-related financial risk metrics as lenders face mounting climate exposure scrutiny across portfolios.
Sompo acquires Service Insurance to bulk up US workers' comp footprint; Duck Creek buys Send Technology to embed agentic AI in underwriting.
Wildfire acceleration is overwhelming California evacuation infrastructure, creating liability and operational headwinds for carriers and municipalities.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Four stories spanning BNPL regulation, mega-payments M&A, fintech expansion, and AI oversight shape the specialty finance landscape.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for US banking license, signaling major pivot from BNPL into regulated banking operations.
JPMorgan and BofA in talks to acquire Fiserv's debit network; deal size and timeline undisclosed but signals consolidation in payments infrastructure.
LemFi clears UK regulatory approval to acquire Wealth8, expanding diaspora-focused fintech into wealth management.
FCA publishes AI review positioning algorithmic tools as innovation driver in retail financial services, setting regulatory tone for sector adoption.

PE & Deals.lede: Institutional capital remains robust despite headwinds—GPIF grows PE 26%, Eurazeo closes oversized secondaries fund, while discipline replaces easy-money returns.

bullets.text: GPIF deployed $1.4bn into PE (26% YoY growth) despite 'challenging' environment, signaling sustained appetite from mega-LPs for illiquids.
StepStone CEO: PE's model survives post-easy-money era, but uniform returns myth is dead—selectivity and discipline now separate winners from also-rans.
Eurazeo closes €2.3bn secondaries fund (2x Fund IV), exceeding target and validating strong LP appetite for secondaries amid market normalization.
Novo Holdings ($36bn AUM) exploring dedicated PE GP allocations and Asia expansion; Chinese PE firms repositioning after regulatory pressure, creating disciplined deal environment.

Markets & Macro.lede: 4 stories spanning credit tightening, money market structure shifts, central bank easing, and emerging market policy challenges.

bullets.text: HSBC pulling back from private credit renewals signals risk appetite shrinking in a $1.2T+ market—watch for peer contagion.
OIS spreads have collapsed post-2020, killing a 22-year arbitrage that funded banks' overnight operations—structural money market shift with lasting P&L implications.
Bank of England easing CCyB rules to unlock lending capacity; proactive de-risking ahead of potential stress.
UK DB pensions in surplus for first time in years—policy headwind brewing around contribution holidays and benefit enhancement demands.

Newsletters.lede: 5 stories spanning crypto convictions breaking down, regulatory headwinds on novel ETFs, tax planning wins for HNW clients, and AI giants racing to IPO amid pricing pressure.

bullets.text: MicroStrategy's bitcoin fortress cracking: BTC down 52% from Oct peak ($126K→$60K) is forcing a reckoning on the 'never sell' thesis that defined the strategy.
SEC tightening the ETF screws: Regulators are actively scrutinizing novel and leveraged ETF launches, signaling incoming friction for product approvals.
IRS gift-tax reprieve for Trump Accounts: New reclassification as current-interest gifts expands tax-efficient wealth transfer options for HNW clients.
OpenAI and Anthropic sprinting to IPO while customers rebel against per-token pricing—both growth and margin pressure converging in AI capital markets.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four substantive takes on market efficiency, compensation structures, and tech adoption—plus a prediction markets story showing real-time signal aggregation.

bullets.text: Government equity stakes in tech (Deutsche Telekom example) misalign incentives and suppress innovation—a cautionary note on state ownership models.
Labor's income share decline may overstate wage erosion: equity comp and pass-throughs counted as capital gains mask what's really deferred compensation.
Prediction markets moved a Maine Democratic primary dropout odds from 9% to 96% in one day on scandal—Cowen argues 'decision markets' could improve institutional choices.

all.lede: Vestmark's TAMP platform hits $50B AUM milestone on retail UMA launch, while Klarna pursues U.S. banking regulation and JPM/BofA eye Fiserv's debit network.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP AUM surged 5x past $50B after launching standalone retail UMA with tax-loss harvesting; T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, and VanEck now funneling referrals directly.
Klarna applies for ILC charter with FDIC, signaling major pivot from buy-now-pay-later into regulated deposit-taking and lending products under U.S. banking supervision.
JPMorgan and BofA in active talks to acquire Fiserv's debit payments network; no valuation disclosed yet.
